title: Confounding and Interaction
text: Confounding asks whether adjustment changes exposure effect. Interaction asks whether exposure effect differs across another variable. checks screening aids viewing organising results. Use DAGs, subject-matter knowledge, study design decide variables confounders effect modifiers. Automated change--estimate interaction checks used sole basis model adjustment.

title: Confounding and Interaction
text: practical workflow : Use DAGs, prior literature, study design list important variables. Use identify_confounder() organise screening evidence candidate confounders effect modifiers. Use interaction_models() planned interaction hypothesis. interaction important, consider stratified reporting stratified_uni_reg() stratified_multi_reg().
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW"))   )

title: Confounding and Interaction
text: Use method = change model-based change--estimate method. Use method = mh method = Mantel-Haenszel appropriate. output intentionally tidy rather publication-first.
code: confounder_check <- identify_confounder(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposure = "smoke",   potential_confounder = c("race", "ht"),   approach = logit,   method = both )  confounder_check$summary ## # A tibble: 2 × 13 ##   exposure candidate crude_est adjusted_est mh_est percent_change ##   <chr>    <chr>         <dbl>        <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl> ## 1 smoke    race           2.02         3.05   3.09          51.0  ## 2 smoke    ht             2.02         2.04   2.03           0.78 ## # ℹ 7 more variables: percent_change_model <dbl>, percent_change_mh <dbl>, ## #   is_confounder <lgl>, interaction_p <dbl>, is_effect_modifier <lgl>, ## #   decision <chr>, recommendation <chr>

title: Confounding and Interaction
text: interaction_models() compares models without interaction term. deliberately model-based uses LRT Wald, Mantel-Haenszel. Use interaction term planned supported clinical, causal, subject-matter reasoning.
code: interaction_check <- interaction_models(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposure = "smoke",   effect_modifier = "race",   covariates = c("age", "lwt"),   approach = logit,   test = LRT,   format = gt )  interaction_check$table

title: Confounding and Interaction
text: identify_confounder(): $summary, $table, $details. interaction_models(): $summary, $table, $p_value, $decision, fitted model objects. Use subject-matter knowledge outputs. functions support interpretation; replace study design.

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
text: Make output look like belongs final report. Rename labels, merge tables, save tables plots.
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW"))   )  birthwt_desc <- descriptive_table(   birthwt_data,   exposures = c("age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui"),   by = "low" ) birthwt_uni <- uni_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui"),   approach = logit ) birthwt_multi <- multi_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui"),   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt", "race"),   approach = logit )

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
text: Adjusted regression low birth weight
code: birthwt_custom <- modify_table(   birthwt_multi,   variable_labels = c(     smoke = "Smoking during pregnancy",     ht = "Hypertension",     ui = "Uterine irritability"   ),   header_labels = c(estimate = "Adjusted OR", p.value = "P"),   caption = "Adjusted regression for low birth weight" )  birthwt_custom$table

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
code: birthwt_merged <- merge_tables(   birthwt_desc,   birthwt_uni,   birthwt_multi,   spanners = c("Descriptive", "Crude", "Adjusted") )  birthwt_merged$table

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
text: destination supplied, save helpers use temporary folder.
code: save_table(birthwt_merged, filename = "birthwt-table", format = html)  birthwt_plot <- plot_reg(   birthwt_multi,   title = "Adjusted Regression for Low Birth Weight" )  save_plot(birthwt_plot, filename = "birthwt-forest", format = png)

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
text: Use format = flextable destination Word.
code: birthwt_multi_ft <- multi_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui"),   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt", "race"),   approach = logit,   format = flextable )  save_docx(   tables = list(birthwt_multi_ft),   filename = "birthwt-report",   titles = "Adjusted Regression" )

title: Customize, Merge, and Export
text: modify_table(): changed labels, caption, caveat. merge_tables(): $table, $table_display, $footnotes. save_table(), save_plot(), save_docx(): returned file paths.

title: Descriptive Tables
text: Start table people can actually read. descriptive_table() creates publication-ready summaries continuous, categorical, mixed exposure sets.
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW")),     ptl_cat = factor(ifelse(ptl > 0, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes")),     ftv_cat = factor(case_when(       ftv == 0 ~ "None",       ftv == 1 ~ "One",       ftv >= 2 ~ "Two or more"     ), levels = c("None", "One", "Two or more"))   )  birthwt_exposures <- c(   "age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat", "ftv_cat" )

title: Descriptive Tables
text: Use column percentages table grouped outcome another column.
code: desc_column <- descriptive_table(   data = birthwt_data,   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   by = "low",   percent = column,   show_overall = last,   theme = clinical )  desc_column$table

title: Descriptive Tables
text: Use row percentages question exposure level distributed across groups.
code: descriptive_table(   data = birthwt_data,   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   by = "low",   percent = row,   show_overall = first,   show_missing = no,   theme = striped )$table

title: Descriptive Tables
code: descriptive_table(   data = birthwt_data,   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   by = "low",   percent = column,   format = flextable )$table

title: Regression Tables
text: Go fitted models publication-ready tables without hand-formatting effect estimates. gtregression supports logistic, log-binomial, Poisson, robust Poisson, negative binomial, linear regression.
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW")),     ptl_cat = factor(ifelse(ptl > 0, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes")),     ftv_cat = factor(case_when(       ftv == 0 ~ "None",       ftv == 1 ~ "One",       ftv >= 2 ~ "Two or more"     ), levels = c("None", "One", "Two or more"))   )  birthwt_exposures <- c(   "age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat", "ftv_cat" )

title: Regression Tables
text: uni_reg() fits one model per exposure returns table ready reports.
code: birthwt_uni <- uni_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   approach = logit,   theme = clinical )  birthwt_uni$table

title: Regression Tables
text: multi_reg() can fit exposures one model, fit one adjusted model per exposure using adjustment set. adjustment variables recorded table footnote result ready manuscript-style reporting.
code: birthwt_multi <- multi_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat", "ftv_cat"),   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt", "race"),   approach = logit,   theme = striped )  birthwt_multi$table

title: Regression Tables
text: Switch approach change estimand.
code: uni_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat"),   approach = logbinomial )$table

title: Regression Tables
text: Linear regression outputs beta coefficients keeps diagnostics $reg_check.
code: birthwt_linear <- multi_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "bwt",   exposures = c("age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui"),   approach = linear )  birthwt_linear$table

title: Regression Tables
text: $table: publication-ready table. $table_body: numeric estimates behind display. $models: fitted model objects. $model_summaries: model-level summaries. $reg_check: diagnostics linear models.

title: Stratified Analysis
text: Stratified regression repeats analysis inside subgroup places results side side. useful association may look different across groups.
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW")),     ptl_cat = factor(ifelse(ptl > 0, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes"))   )

title: Stratified Analysis
code: strata_uni <- stratified_uni_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("age", "lwt", "smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat"),   stratifier = "race",   approach = logit,   theme = clinical )  strata_uni$table

title: Stratified Analysis
text: Use adjust_for exposure adjusted variables within stratum.
code: strata_multi <- stratified_multi_reg(   data = birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat"),   stratifier = "race",   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt"),   approach = logit,   theme = striped )  strata_multi$table

title: Stratified Analysis
text: $table: rendered side--side table. $table_display: wide data used build table. $models: fitted models stratum. $reg_check: diagnostics linear models.

title: Visualise Regression Results
text: Regression tables evidence. Plots vibe check. Use plot_reg(), plot_reg_combine(), forest_df(), forest_reg() make results easier scan.
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW")),     ptl_cat = factor(ifelse(ptl > 0, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No", "Yes")),     ftv_cat = factor(case_when(       ftv == 0 ~ "None",       ftv == 1 ~ "One",       ftv >= 2 ~ "Two or more"     ), levels = c("None", "One", "Two or more"))   )  birthwt_exposures <- c(   "age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat", "ftv_cat" )  birthwt_desc <- descriptive_table(   birthwt_data,   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   by = "low",   show_overall = last ) birthwt_uni <- uni_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = birthwt_exposures,   approach = logit ) birthwt_multi <- multi_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui", "ptl_cat", "ftv_cat"),   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt", "race"),   approach = logit )

title: Visualise Regression Results
code: plot_reg(   birthwt_multi,   title = "Adjusted Regression for Low Birth Weight" )

title: Visualise Regression Results
code: plot_reg_combine(   tbl_uni = birthwt_uni,   tbl_multi = birthwt_multi,   title_uni = "Crude Effects",   title_multi = "Adjusted Effects" )

title: Visualise Regression Results
text: forest_df() prepares data. forest_reg() draws forest table.
code: forest_data <- forest_df(   uni = birthwt_uni,   multi = birthwt_multi,   desc = birthwt_desc )  forest_reg(forest_data, quiet = TRUE)

title: Visualise Regression Results
text: plot_reg() returns ggplot. plot_reg_combine() returns combined ggplot. forest_df() returns plotting data frame. forest_reg() returns plot, data, input_data, meta.

title: gtregression
text: gtregression helps move model manuscript: fit regression models, produce clean tables, visualise estimates, merge outputs, export results without hand-formatting every coefficient. supports logistic, log-binomial, Poisson, robust Poisson, negative binomial, linear, adjusted, stratified regression workflows. DescribeBuild baseline tables grouped summaries. ModelFit crude, adjusted, stratified regressions. VisualiseCreate plots forest tables estimates. ExportSave polished tables, figures, Word outputs.

title: gtregression
text: Objects stay inspectable:
code: library(gtregression) library(dplyr)  data("data_birthwt", package = "gtregression")  birthwt_data <- data_birthwt |>   mutate(     race = factor(race, levels = c(1, 2, 3),                   labels = c("White", "Black", "Other")),     smoke = factor(smoke, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ht = factor(ht, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     ui = factor(ui, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),     low = factor(low, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Normal BW", "Low BW"))   )  exposures <- c("age", "lwt", "race", "smoke", "ht", "ui")  desc <- descriptive_table(   birthwt_data,   exposures = exposures,   by = "low",   percent = column,   show_overall = last )  uni <- uni_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = exposures,   approach = logit )  multi <- multi_reg(   birthwt_data,   outcome = "low",   exposures = c("smoke", "ht", "ui"),   adjust_for = c("age", "lwt", "race"),   approach = logit )  plot_reg(multi, title = "Adjusted Regression for Low Birth Weight") desc$table uni$table multi$table multi$models

title: Check collinearity using VIF for fitted models — check_collinearity
text: multivariable models, tibble single fitted model present, named list tibbles multiple fitted models present. format = "gt" format = "flextable", leaf tibbles converted formatted tables preserving list nesting. tibble contains: Variable Model term. VIF Variance inflation factor. multi-degree--freedom   terms, adjusted GVIF: GVIF^(1 / (2 * Df)). Interpretation Simple interpretation based common cut   points: collinearity, moderate, high. univariate models, error raised indicating VIF applicable.

what: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
title: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
text: Assesses model convergence provides diagnostics exposure (univariate mode) full model (multivariable mode), depending regression approach used.

title: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
code: check_convergence(   data,   exposures,   outcome,   approach = "logit",   multivariate = FALSE,   format = c("tibble", "gt", "flextable") )

title: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
text: data data frame containing dataset. exposures character vector predictor variable names. multivariate = FALSE, exposure assessed separately. multivariate = TRUE, exposures included together. outcome character string specifying outcome variable. approach character string specifying regression approach. One :   "logit", "logbinomial", "poisson",   "robpoisson", "negbin". multivariate Logical. TRUE, checks convergence multivariable model;   otherwise, performs checks univariate model. format Output format. One "tibble", "gt", "flextable". default "tibble" returns original data-frame style output.

title: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
text: data frame, gt_tbl, flextable summarizing convergence diagnostics, including: Exposure Name exposure variable. Model regression approach used. Converged TRUE model converged successfully;    FALSE otherwise. Max.prob. Maximum predicted probability   fitted value dataset.

title: Check Convergence for a Regression Model — check_convergence
text: robpoisson, predicted probabilities (fitted values) may exceed 1, acceptable estimating risk ratios interpreted actual probabilities. function useful identifying convergence issues, especially "logbinomial" models, often fail converge.

title: gtregression 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-18 Initial release gtregression. Added support logit, logbinomial, linear, Poisson, roboisson, negative binomial models. Added functions confounder identification, model selection, diagnostics, plotting, saving output. Added forest plot functions (plot_reg, plot_reg_combine) Improved documentation homepage

title: gtregression 1.1.0 — 2025-09-07
text: Shiny GUI modularized (import, descriptive, regression, advanced). Reproducible-code panes tables/models. ## Changed Improved validation helpers; clearer error messages. ## Fixed Stratified combine UI; PDF/PNG export reliability.
